idea.title: Comet Tether
idea.hook: You're a comet with a finite number of tethers — lasso a planet and its gravity whips you into orbit, release to slingshot, and thread your way to the exit gate without crashing.
idea.prototype: 2-body gravity field on canvas. Comet drifts with momentum; tap a planet within range to tether (capture into a tight orbit), tap again to release at the chosen tangent for a slingshot. Reach the exit gate using a budget of tethers. Controls: tap planet to grab/release. Win: pass through exit gate (6 levels). Lose: out of tethers before the gate, or collide with a body. 3 lives.
idea.mutation_of: poki Stickman Hook (grapple-swing) + Drift Boss momentum + gravity-puzzle space
idea.why_novel: the pivot is a gravity well that actively curves your trajectory, so capture/release is an orbital-mechanics decision not rope-timing; distinct from shipped Pendulum Sniper fixed-arc aiming
idea.lose_condition: You run out of tethers before reaching the exit gate, OR your comet collides with a planet. 3 lives; lives==0 -> GAMEOVER.

synthesizer_brief: physics_action family, daily CTR EXPERIMENT slot (meme_ip_bait archetype): reference-led, glossy toy-plastic cartoon comet with an expressive face and vivid tail whipping around a loud cartoon planet on a glowing tether arc. Core mechanic is a constrained-orbit slingshot: grab snaps the comet to a tight circular orbit, release converts the orbital tangent to free-flight momentum. Non-timer pressure via tether budget + 3 lives; level-based progression (6 levels) with more bodies and tighter routes. Mobile-first portrait.
